_comment: v0.4 scoring rubric. Section 11 of v0.3-design.md, revised by the data-science pass (docs/v0.4-design.md). semantic_fit is the dense-embedding signal (profile centroid vs JD), computed locally with bge-small, zero tokens. The lexical dimensions remain as cheap, explainable signals. Weights are renormalized over whichever dimensions are present, so the total is always in [0,1]. Weights live here so they are tunable without code edits; the eval harness is how you tune them.

semantic_fit.description: Cosine similarity between the candidate's profile centroid (mean of skill and bullet embeddings) and the JD embedding. Captures role, skill, and domain alignment semantically, including synonyms the lexical signals miss.

skills_overlap.description: Explicit skill recall: how many of the user's skills/tech appear literally in the JD body, normalized by a target hit count. The ATS-literal complement to semantic_fit.

role_title.description: Best semantic match between the JD title and the user's target role_families (cosine when an embedder is available, token overlap as fallback).

company_tier.description: Boost for dream_companies; neutral for unknown; zero for exclusions.

location_remote.description: Match against target locations or 'remote' indicators in the JD location.

red_flags.description: Penalty when an exclusion term appears in the JD body or title. Inverse: 1.0 means clean.

calibration._comment: Raw totals are mapped to interpretable bands by percentile across the scored batch (no labels needed). Cutoffs are quantiles of the batch score distribution.
